title: Workflow & journey registry + checker + 'workflow-at-a-time' rule
business_reason: Coding feature-by-feature across unrelated chains loses context and invites hallucination. The founder provided a workflow-autogen script (versa_workflow_autogen_validator.py) to enforce 'code one end-to-end workflow chain at a time'. The idea is right but the script targeted a different artifact layout and would have become a PARALLEL source of truth. Ported it repo-native: a single workflow dictionary that references our real modules + e2e specs, plus a thin checker that derives completion status from the repo.

design.registry: spec/WORKFLOW_REGISTRY.json — the 16 end-to-end chains (WF-001..WF-016) mapped to OUR module ids + real tests/e2e/*.spec.ts. SINGLE-SOURCE: it references modules/specs, never restates effects/screens.
design.checker: _validation/check_workflows.py — validates every referenced module + e2e spec + dependency EXISTS (exit 1 on drift, so the registry can't reference fake things), DERIVES per-workflow status (built/partial/planned/covered-by-deps) from tests/e2e (covered-by-deps only for an explicit aggregate workflow), and writes reports/WORKFLOW_STATUS.md naming the next chain in dependency order.
design.rule: BUILD_PROCESS.md step 0 + PRINCIPLES P0.11: code one chain end-to-end (all gates + P0.10 UI) to 'built' before the next; pick by execution order.
design.decision: Deleted the founder's standalone Python generator (backed up) to avoid a second drifting source of truth alongside CANONICAL_DATA_MODEL.json + chains.json + the generators.

param_14.question: How do we avoid bitty, context-losing coding and track end-to-end chain completion?
param_14.answer: A single workflow dictionary (spec/WORKFLOW_REGISTRY.json) lists the 16 business chains mapped to our modules + e2e specs; check_workflows.py derives each chain's status from tests/e2e and names the next chain to build in dependency order. The loop now mandates finishing one chain end-to-end (all gates incl. UI) before starting the next.
param_14.answer_source: founder's versa_workflow_autogen_validator.py (adapted); our spec/modules + tests/e2e as ground truth
param_14.security_implications: none (process tooling); reduces the chance of shipping API-only/UI-less or partial chains.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: check_workflows.py could later assert module service + UI page presence per workflow (deeper than e2e-spec presence); WORKFLOW_STATUS feeds SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP.
param_14.assumptions: an e2e spec's presence is a reasonable proxy for a chain being journey-built (consistent with the §16 gate).
